title: trifolium × retyezaticum
intro: Trifolium × retyezaticum, also known as Retz's clover or Retz's trefoil, is a rare hybrid species of clover, resulting from a cross between Trifolium retusum and Trifolium tomentosum. The plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Trifolium × retyezaticum include its ability to support kidney function, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Trifolium × retyezaticum can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
